  • The Global Picture: Where Are Young Adults Dying?
  • Gender Gap: Are Young Men Dying More?
  • Does Wealth Save Lives?
  • Trends Over Time
  • Conclusion

Youth on the Edge: A Global Look at Deaths Among Young Adults (Ages 20-24)

Author

Amalu Thomas

Published

April 14, 2026

Introduction

“Every young person lost is a future never realised.”

Each year, hundreds of thousands of young adults aged 20 to 24 die before reaching their prime. This report draws on UNICEF data to explore where, when, and why these deaths occur, and what the data tells us about global inequality.

The Global Picture: Where Are Young Adults Dying?

Code 
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
import numpy as np
from plotnine import *
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

ind2 = pd.read_csv("unicef_indicator_2.csv")
meta = pd.read_csv("unicef_metadata.csv")

recent = ind2[ind2["time_period"] >= 2018].copy()
map_data = (
    recent[recent["sex"] == "Total"]
    .groupby(["country", "alpha_3_code"], as_index=False)["obs_value"]
    .mean()
    .rename(columns={"obs_value": "avg_deaths"})
)

world = gpd.read_file("https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/110m/cultural/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip")
world = world.rename(columns={"ADM0_A3": "alpha_3_code"})
world_merged = world.merge(map_data, on="alpha_3_code", how="left")

(
    ggplot(world_merged)
    + geom_map(aes(fill="avg_deaths"), color="white", size=0.1)
    + scale_fill_gradient(low="#fff5eb", high="#d94701", na_value="#eeeeee", name="Avg Deaths")
    + labs(
        title="Average Annual Deaths of Young Adults (Ages 20-24)",
        subtitle="Per country, averaged 2018-2023. Grey = no data",
        caption="Source: UNICEF"
    )
    + theme_void()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(size=13, face="bold"),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(size=9),
        legend_position="bottom"
    )
)

Gender Gap: Are Young Men Dying More?

Code 
top_countries = (
    ind2[ind2["sex"] == "Total"]
    .groupby("country", as_index=False)["obs_value"]
    .sum()
    .nlargest(15, "obs_value")["country"]
    .tolist()
)

bar_data = (
    ind2[
        (ind2["country"].isin(top_countries)) &
        (ind2["sex"].isin(["Male", "Female"])) &
        (ind2["time_period"] >= 2015)
    ]
    .groupby(["country", "sex"], as_index=False)["obs_value"]
    .mean()
)

(
    ggplot(bar_data, aes(x="reorder(country, -obs_value)", y="obs_value", fill="sex"))
    + geom_col(position="dodge", width=0.7)
    + scale_fill_manual(values={"Male": "#2166ac", "Female": "#d6604d"}, name="Sex")
    + coord_flip()
    + labs(
        title="Young Adult Deaths by Gender",
        subtitle="Top 15 countries, average 2015-2023",
        x="Country",
        y="Average Annual Deaths",
        caption="Source: UNICEF"
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(face="bold", size=12),
        axis_text_y=element_text(size=8)
    )
)

Does Wealth Save Lives?

Code 
ind_agg = (
    ind2[ind2["sex"] == "Total"]
    .groupby(["alpha_3_code", "time_period"], as_index=False)["obs_value"]
    .sum()
    .rename(columns={"time_period": "year", "obs_value": "deaths"})
)

merged = ind_agg.merge(
    meta[["alpha_3_code", "year", "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)", "Population, total"]],
    on=["alpha_3_code", "year"],
    how="inner"
).dropna(subset=["GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)", "deaths"])

merged["gdp_log"] = np.log10(merged["GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)"] + 1)
merged["deaths_per_million"] = (merged["deaths"] / merged["Population, total"]) * 1000000
scatter_data = merged[merged["deaths_per_million"] < 5000]

(
    ggplot(scatter_data, aes(x="gdp_log", y="deaths_per_million"))
    + geom_point(alpha=0.3, color="#2166ac", size=1.5)
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", color="#d94701", se=True)
    + labs(
        title="GDP per Capita vs. Youth Deaths per Million",
        subtitle="Each point = one country-year. Log scale on X axis",
        x="GDP per Capita (log10)",
        y="Deaths per Million (ages 20-24)",
        caption="Source: UNICEF and World Bank"
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(plot_title=element_text(face="bold", size=12))
)

Trends Over Time

Code 
time_data = (
    ind2[ind2["sex"] == "Total"]
    .groupby("time_period", as_index=False)["obs_value"]
    .sum()
    .rename(columns={"time_period": "year", "obs_value": "total_deaths"})
)

(
    ggplot(time_data, aes(x="year", y="total_deaths"))
    + geom_line(color="#2166ac", size=1.2)
    + geom_point(color="#d94701", size=2)
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", linetype="dashed", color="grey", se=False)
    + labs(
        title="Global Youth Deaths (Ages 20-24) Over Time",
        subtitle="Total annual deaths worldwide, 1990-2023",
        x="Year",
        y="Total Deaths",
        caption="Source: UNICEF"
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(plot_title=element_text(face="bold", size=12))
)

Conclusion

This analysis reveals four key findings:

  1. Geography matters - Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia bear a disproportionate burden
  2. Gender matters - Young men die at higher rates in virtually every country
  3. Wealth matters - Higher GDP per capita is linked to lower youth mortality
  4. Progress is real but unequal - Global deaths are declining, but not fast enough in the poorest nations

Data Sources: UNICEF Global Data Repository | World Bank Development Indicators
